Rescued Resolve.prerequisites: Snagger, an owned Pokémon with Loyalty 4 who you’ve Snagged
Rescued Resolve.target: Your Pokémon with Loyalty 4 who you’ve Snagged.
Rescued Resolve.effect: Whenever a Target deals damage, they deal an additional X damage, where X is your SPD modifier.

Rescuer.prerequisites: Snagger, an owned Pokémon with Loyalty 3 who you’ve Snagged
Rescuer.effect: When you are using Snag! on a Pokémon with Loyalty 0 or 1, it will have at least loyalty 1 when captured.

Shadow Berserk.prerequisites: Snagger, a Pokémon who was once another Trainer’s Pokémon
Shadow Berserk.target: Your Owned Pokémon with Loyalty 0, 1 or 2.
Shadow Berserk.effect: The target immediately uses the Move Submission, but when applying Weakness/ Resistances, treat Submission as having no Elemental Type, hitting for neutral damage. The target may not perform another Move during this round of combat. Using Shadow Berserk makes a Pokémon more prone to disliking its trainer.

Shadow Blaze.prerequisites: Snagger, a Fire-Type Pokémon who was once another Trainer’s Pokémon
Shadow Blaze.target: Your Owned Pokémon with Loyalty 0, 1 or 2.
Shadow Blaze.effect: The target immediately uses the Move Flamethrower, but when applying Weakness/ Resistances, treat Flamethrower as having no Elemental Type, hitting for neutral damage. The target may not perform another Move during this round of combat. Using a Shadow Blaze makes a Pokémon more prone to disliking its trainer.

Shadow Bolt.prerequisites: Snagger, an Electric-Type Pokémon who was once another Trainer’s Pokémon
Shadow Bolt.target: Your Owned Pokémon with Loyalty 0, 1 or 2.
Shadow Bolt.effect: The target immediately uses the Move Thunderbolt, but when applying Weakness/ Resistances, treat Thunderbolt as having no Elemental Type, hitting for neutral damage. The target may not perform another Move during this round of combat. Using Shadow Bolt makes a Pokémon more prone to disliking its trainer.

Shadow Chill.prerequisites: Snagger, an Ice-Type Pokémon who was once another Trainer’s Pokémon
Shadow Chill.target: Your Owned Pokémon with Loyalty 0, 1 or 2.
Shadow Chill.effect: The target immediately uses the Move Ice Beam, but when applying Weakness/Resistances, treat Ice Beam as having no Elemental Type, hitting for neutral damage. The target may not perform another Move during this round of combat. Using Shadow Chill makes a Pokémon more prone to disliking its trainer.

Shadow Smash.prerequisites: Snagger, a Fighting-Type Pokémon who was once another Trainer’s Pokémon
Shadow Smash.target: Your Owned Pokémon with Loyalty 0, 1 or 2.
Shadow Smash.effect: The target immediately uses the Move Brick Break, but when applying Weakness/Resistances, treat Brick Break as having no Elemental Type, hitting for neutral damage. The target may not perform another Move during this round of combat. Using a Shadow Smash makes a Pokémon more prone to disliking its trainer.

Snag Machine Upgrade.effect: The Feature Snag! now has “Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day.” Instead of “Daily.”

Subtle Break.prerequisites: Snagger, a Pokémon who was once another Trainer’s Pokémon
Subtle Break.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. If you roll 20 or higher, you may target the Pokémon with Loyalty 2 for the next 2 rounds with Snag!

Big Game Hunting.effect: Name the Target as your Quarry. When calculating the Catch Rate of your Quarry, their level is considered to be reduced by X. X is equal to half your SPD modifier. You may only have one Quarry at a time, and may only declare or change your Quarry at the start of a round.

Lasso.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day Target: A Pokémon or Trainer Effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier
Lasso.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. On a roll of 12 or higher, the target is Lassoed. A Lassoed target cannot move than 6 meters away from you. A Lassoed Pokémon may attempt to end the effect by forfeiting its turn, if it is not Confused, Asleep, or Knocked Out. It then rolls 1d20 plus their Power Skill. On a roll of 14 or higher, the Pokémon breaks free. If targeting a trainer, use half their ATK modifier as their Power Skill.

Big Game Hunting +.effect: Name the Target as your Quarry. When calculating the Catch Rate of your Quarry, their level is considered to be reduced by X. X is equal to your SPD modifier. You may only have one Quarry at a time, and may only declare or change your Quarry at the start of a round. This feature replaces Big Game Hunting.

Bring’em Down.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day Target: A Lassoed Pokémon or Trainer
Bring’em Down.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. On a roll of X or higher, the target Pokémon becomes Trapped for Y rounds, where Y is equal to half your SPD modifier. X is equal to 10 plus the Pokémon’s Power Skill, or 10 plus half the Trainer’s ATK modifier.

False Strike.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day Trigger: You deal damage to your Quarry
False Strike.trigger: You deal damage to your Quarry.
False Strike.effect: When you make attacks with an Arms Feature that would reduce a target to 0 HP or less, you may choose to have the target instead remain at 1 HP.

Pheromone Spray.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day Target: A Pokémon Effect: The target Pokémon becomes Infatuated with the nearest Pokémon of the opposite gender
Pheromone Spray.effect: The target Pokémon becomes Infatuated with the nearest Pokémon of the opposite gender.

Right There!.prerequisites: Trapper, a Pokémon with a Trap Move
Right There!.target: A Pokémon trapped by one of your Pokémon
Right There!.effect: When targeting a Pokémon for capture, subtract 10 from your roll if your Pokémon is currently Trapping the target.

Stun Trap.effect: Place a marker on the field adjacent to you. If a Pokémon or trainer crosses over that spot on the ground they trigger the Stun Trap. The Pokémon or person who triggered the Trap is Paralyzed.

Set for Capture.trigger: You successfully use Bringem Down on a Lassoed Pokémon.
Set for Capture.effect: The target Pokémon’s catch rate is increased by 15 for the remainder of the time they are Trapped.

Sweet Trap.prerequisites: Trapper, a Pokémon with Sweet Scent or the Alluring Skill

Tranquilizer.prerequisites: Trapper, a Pokémon with Sleep Powder or Spore
Tranquilizer.effect: Use the move Yawn on a single target within 30 meters. After successfully falling asleep due to the effects of Tranquilizer, the target becomes immune to Tranquilizer for 6 hours.

Yank!.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day Trigger: A Lassoed Pokémon attempts to use a Move
Yank!.trigger: A Lassoed Pokémon attempts to use a Move.
Yank!.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. On a result of 16 or higher, the Pokémon takes damage equal to its STAB value, ignoring stats, and may not use any moves this turn. Otherwise, the Pokémon acts normally.

Natural Grace.effect: Your Pokémon Learn +20% the amount of experience they would gain normally from contests. If you win a Contest, you gain an additional +15% the amount of prize money you normally would.

Style and Poise.effect: Your Pokémon may have a total number of combined Contest Stats equal to 24. Furthermore, they may gain the effects of any number of Poffins until they reach this maximum. If a Pokémon is traded away to a non-Coordinator, and the Pokémon has more then 20 points of Contest Stats, they do not lose those stats, but their new trainers may not have access to more than 20 Contest Points per Contest. Some Coordinator Features have multiple uses, one for Contests and one for nonContests. They maintain the same prerequisites and frequencies but activate differently based on when they are activated; whether or not it is used during a Contest. Some of these non-Contest Effects allow the Coordinator to perform Pokémon Moves, while others allow their Pokémon to perform them even if they do not know them.

Catch Up!.effect: Replace a keyword on a Contest Move performed by your Pokémon with Catching Up. You may use Catch Up only once per contest. Battle Effect: Use the move Helping Hand.

Crowd’s Cheers.effect: Replace a Contest Keyword on a Contest Move performed by your Pokémon with Crowd Pleaser for one round of the Contest. Battle Effect: Use the move Encore.

Electrifying Performance.effect: Add the Contest Keyword Incredible to a Contest Move performed by the Pokémon this Round. Battle Effect: The target immediately uses the Move Lock-On as a Free Action. During the target’s turn it may not use any of the following Moves: Fissure, Guillotine, Horn Drill or Sheer Cold. You may still roll to determine additional effects or Critical Hit. This must be declared before the Accuracy Check is made.

Judge Blinders.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Judge Blinders.effect: Replace a Contest Keyword on a Contest Move performed by your Pokémon with Special Attention. You may use Judge Blinders only once per contest. Battle Effect: The target immediately uses the Move Follow Me as its Move Action, it may still shift during its turn during this round of combat.

Lead Act.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Lead Act.effect: Replace a Contest Keyword on a Contest Move performed by your Pokémon with Quick Set. You may use Lead Act only once per contest. Battle Effect: The target immediately uses the Move Me First as its Move Action, it may still shift during its turn during this round of combat.

Patience.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Patience.effect: Replace a Contest Keyword on a Contest Move performed by your Pokémon with Slow Set. You may use Patience only once per contest. Battle Effect: Use the move Quash.

Perfect Performance.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Perfect Performance.target: Your Pokémon who just received First or Second place in a contest.
Perfect Performance.effect: The target gains +30% experience from the Contest if they came in Second place. They gain +80% experience instead if they came in First place.

Voltage Freeze.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Voltage Freeze.effect: Replace a Contest Keyword on a Contest Move performed by your Pokémon with either Excitement or Hold That Thought this round. You may use Voltage Freeze only once per contest. Battle Effect: Your Pokémon gains the keyword “Reliable” on the next Battle Frequency Move it performs during this encounter.

Voltage Freeze +.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Voltage Freeze +.effect: Replace a Contest Keyword on a Contest Move performed by your Pokémon with either Excitement, Hold That Thought, or both. You may use Voltage Freeze+ only once per contest. Battle Effect: Your Pokémon gains the keyword “Reliable” on the next Battle or Center Frequency moves it performs. This feature replaces Voltage Freeze+

You Call That Impressive?.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
You Call That Impressive?.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. On a result of 13 or higher, add the Contest Keyword Seen Nothing Yet to your Pokémon’s Contest Move for this round. Battle Effect: The target gains one of these effects based on which Move it will be using during this round of combat: If the Move has the keyword Blast or Burst, increase the size of the Blast/Burst by 2. If the Move does not have either of those keywords and is Ranged, increase its range by 4. If the Move is Melee, increase the target’s Speed Skills by 4.

Stage Notation.effect: Once per contest, when of your Pokémon performs a Song Move, roll +1d6 during that appeal. Once per contest, when of your Pokémon performs a Dance Move, roll +1d6 during that appeal.

Writing New Parts.target: A Move on a Pokémon’s Move List
Writing New Parts.effect: Writing New Parts makes the target Move a “Song Move,” or a “Dance Move,” but only for that Pokémon. Writing New Parts may not make more than 3 different Moves a Song Move or a Dance Move for a single Pokémon. You can make newly learned Moves a Song Move or Dance Move, even if a Pokémon already have 3 total Song Moves and Dance Moves made with Writing New Parts, if an older Song or Dance Move is made to no longer be a Song or Dance Move. Moves can be Song and Dance Moves, but will count as 2, when counting the total of 3 Song/Dance Moves. When counting the 3 Song and Dance Moves, ignore those considered Song/Dance Moves listed in the Choreographer Mechanics. Moves with the keyword “Dance” are naturally considered “Dance Moves” Moves with the keyword “Sound” are naturally considered “Song Moves.” Natural Song Moves and Dance Moves do not count towards Writing New Parts’ “3.”

Acrobatic Steps.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Acrobatic Steps.effect: For the remainder of the encounter, you have +3 Evasion. Acrobatic Steps may only be used once per encounter.

Benesh Script.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Benesh Script.target: One of your Pokémon performing a Dance Move, with a Melee Range, in battle and another allied Pokémon with a line of sight to your Pokémon.
Benesh Script.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. On a result of 13 or better, the Pokémon you target will perform the same Dance Move that your Pokémon used during their next action. It may choose new targets.

Dazzling The Stage.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Dazzling The Stage.target: One of your Pokémon performing a Song Move or Dance Move in battle.
Dazzling The Stage.effect: Choose another Song Move or Dance Move without a Damage Dice Roll on the target’s Move List. Add its effects to the Song Move or Dance Move the Pokémon is using, then it is Exhausted. Any effects that set a target to a certain HP cannot be used with Dazzling The Stage and any effects that make Hazards or Walls cannot be used with Dazzling The Stage. A Move modified by Dazzling The Stage cannot be combined through the use of Tag Battler Features.

Demiflat Vibrato.effect: When your Pokémon would have any of its Combat Stages lowered by a Move with a Damage Dice Roll used by a foe, the foe must have rolled at least a 19 during accuracy check for it to successfully lower your Pokémon’s Combat Stages. When your Pokémon use a Song Move, anything targeting them during the same round of combat must roll +1 to hit once per encounter.

En Point.effect: When your Pokémon use a Dance Move, they cannot be Pushed or Flinched during that round of Combat. When your Pokémon are Paralyzed, if they use a Dance Move you may treat its next Paralyze check as a 16 once per encounter.

Fortissimo Leap.frequency: Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Fortissimo Leap.target: One of your Pokémon performing a Song Move or Dance Move in battle.
Fortissimo Leap.effect: Your Pokémon uses the Song Move or Dance Move twice during their Move action, then is Exhausted for 2 rounds of Combat. The target cannot be returned to a Poke Ball for 2 rounds of Combat unless has 0 HP or less. If the Pokémon uses a Move with Exhaust, it is Exhausted for an additional 2 turns.

Mirror Staccato.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Mirror Staccato.target: One of your Pokémon performing a Song Move, with at least one target, in battle.
Mirror Staccato.effect: The Song Move gets the effect, “targets must target the user on their next turn.” If your Pokémon is successfully hit during any target’s turn during the next round of combat, reduce the damage by your SDEF and SPD modifiers.

Rising Accompaniment.target: One of your Pokémon performing a Song Move, with no Damage Dice Roll, in battle.
Rising Accompaniment.effect: The Song Move may be used by any allied Pokémon as its Move action for the rest of the round of combat, if, on any allied Pokémon’s turn, they roll 8 or better on 1d20. Do not ignore Frequency for any allied Pokémon who may have used an EOT, Battle or Center Song Move during any previous round of combat. If an allied Pokémon fails the roll to use the Song Move, it may still make its normal Move action.

Teamwork Chorus.effect: During the next round of combat, all allied Trainers and Pokémon within 10 meters of you either deal X more damage while using Moves or Features that have Damage Dice Rolls or take X less damage from Moves or Features that have Damage Dice Rolls. X is your SDEF or SPD modifier, but cannot exceed 5.

Wild Flair.effect: Once per Contest, when one of your Pokémon uses a Dance Move that the Pokémon would get a STAB bonus using the Move during combat, you may raise any single judge’s voltage one level. Battle Effect: Once per encounter, if your Pokémon's Combat Stages would be lowered as a result of an opponent’s Move, ignore that effect.

Cool Advantage.effect: X is your SPD modifier. Add X to your Pokémon’s appearance appeal in the first round of a Cool Contest. Your Pokémon are Cool. All of your Pokémon with Loyalty 2 or higher may be targeted as if they have a Cool Ribbon, but do not actually have one. Your Pokémon may have up to 20 points in their Cool Contest Stat, and burn up to 5 points of its Cool Stat on a single move.

Cool Offense.target: Your Pokémon with a Cool Contest Ribbon
Cool Offense.effect: Mark your Pokémon with Cool Ribbons as “Unleashed.” When adding stats during level up for Unleashed Pokémon, while the Pokémon is still yours, you may add to the Pokémon’s Attack Stat and ignore Base Relation.

Cool Break.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Cool Break.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Cool Ribbon.
Cool Break.effect: The target gains the Mold Breaker Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Mold Breaker, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Cool Defiance.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Cool Defiance.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Cool Ribbon.
Cool Defiance.effect: The target gains the Defiant Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Defiant, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Cool Eye.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Cool Eye.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Cool Ribbon.
Cool Eye.effect: The target gains the Sniper Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Sniper, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Cool Stare.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Cool Stare.target: Your Pokémon or allied Pokémon with a Cool Ribbon.
Cool Stare.effect: The target gains the Intimidate Pokémon ability, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has Intimidate, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled.

Cool’s Allies.prerequisites: Cool Trainer, 1 Beauty Ribbon or 1 Tough Ribbon
Cool’s Allies.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Cool’s Allies.effect: When the target uses a Beauty Move or a Tough Move in a Contest, you may choose to have the Move raise the Voltage. Battle Effect: Once per combat, if the target uses a Move that has a Cool Contest Type, the Move needs -2 to hit.

Cool’s New Allies.prerequisites: Cool Trainer, 1 Smart Ribbon or 1 Cute Ribbon
Cool’s New Allies.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Cool’s New Allies.effect: When the target uses a Smart Move or Cute Move in a Contest, you may choose to have the Move not lower the Voltage. Battle Effect: Once per combat, if the target is attacked, the attacker must role +X to hit where X is your SPD modifier.

Crazy Awesome.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Crazy Awesome.effect: When the target uses a Cool Move, roll an additional 1d4 during the appeal. Battle Effect: When the target uses a Move that has a Cool Contest Type, the Move raises the target’s Attack 1 Combat Stage.

Leaders of The Pack.effect: Your Pokémon with at least 1 Cool Ribbon and a Cool Stat of 10 or higher have the Skill Pack Mon.

Raditude.target: Your Pokémon or an ally’s Pokémon.
Raditude.effect: Roll 1d20 and your SPD modifier. If your result is 16 or greater, you may change the target’s Nature to Desperate, Lonely, Adamant, Naughty or Brave.

Rule of Cool.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Rule of Cool.target: Your Pokémon with at least 15 in their Cool Stat.
Rule of Cool.effect: When the target uses a Move with a Cool Contest Type, that move cannot be Intercepted or Interrupted.

Simple Accessory.effect: After twenty minutes of work, create an Accessory. When worn, the accessory adds +1 Appeal during the Introduction Stage of a Contest.

Sparkles and Glitter.effect: Add +X to the Pokémon’s Appeal during the Introduction Stage, where X is the number of Fashion Designer accessories made by you being worn by the target. Mechanics: Pokémon and Trainers alike can each equip and benefit from a maximum of 3 Accessories. Clothing Piece items count as Accessories, but may only be equipped and activated by Trainers. Conversely, Held-Items can only be equipped and activated by Pokémon, unless the HeldItem is also a Clothing Piece.

Ability Design.effect: After two hours of work, choose a Pokémon Ability one of your Pokémon has, then create an Ability Design Held-Item that may be activated once per day. When activated, the holder gains chosen ability for 2 rounds. You may not copy Wonder Guard with Ability Design

Capability Design.effect: After two hours of work, choose a numbered Skill, then create a Capability Design Held-Item that, when activated, adds +2 to the chosen Skill for five minutes. If you chose the Intelligence or Power Skills, add only +1 for five minutes, and may not raise Intelligence or Power above 7. A Pokémon may not benefit from multiple bonuses to the same Skill from a Capability Design item. Once activated a third time, the Accessory breaks.

Designed Stability.effect: Whenever you create an Accessory or Clothing item that has a limited number of uses, you may spend another three hours working to make the item a Stable Accessory. Stable Accessories and Clothing do not break, but may only be activated once per day.

Designed Volatility.prerequisites: Fashion Designer, created at least 10 items with Fashion Designer Features
Designed Volatility.effect: Whenever you create an Accessory or Clothing item that has a limited number of uses, you may spend another three hours working to make the item a Volatile Accessory. Volatile Accessories do not break after a set number of uses; instead, each time they are activated, you must roll 1d20. If you roll less than 5, the accessory or clothing item loses all its abilities.

Item Stylist.effect: After thirty minutes of work, the Targeted item adds +1 Appeal during the introduction stage of a Contest if equipped. If the targeted item was not already an Accessory, it becomes an Accessory. You may not target the same item multiple times with Item Stylist.

Lucky Tailor.effect: After five hours of work, you create a Lucky Piece, which is a Clothing Item for Trainers. The Lucky Piece may be used to roll 1d8 and add the result to any roll the wearer makes. Once activated a third time, the Lucky Piece breaks. A Trainer may benefit from a Lucky Piece only once each round. In addition, Trainers wearing clothing made with Lucky Tailor get +1 when rolling a check that consults SDEF.

Move Accessory.effect: After four hours of work, choose a Pokémon Move known by a Pokémon you own, then create a Move Accessory that may be activated once per day to simulate the chosen Move while in a Contest. Once activated a third time, the Move Accessory loses all its abilities.

Practical Tailor.effect: After one hour of work, choose a Trainer Stat. You create a Stat Piece, which is a Clothing Piece. When activated, it adds +1 to the chosen stat’s Modifier for five minutes. Once activated a third time, the Stat Piece loses all its abilities. A Trainer may not benefit from multiple pieces of Stat Tailor that add to the same Trainer Stat at the same time.

Style Accessory.effect: After one hour of work, choose a Contest Stat, then create a Type Accessory that, when worn, adds +1 to the appeal roll of any moves of the chosen Contest Type. A Pokémon or Trainer may not benefit from multiple Type Accessories that add to the same Contest Stat.

Style Tailor.effect: After one hour of work, choose a Contest Stat. You create a Type Piece, which is a Clothing Item for trainers. that adds +X to the chosen Contest Stat, where X is your base SDEF modifier at the time you make the Item. These Contest Stat points may be burned when using a move through a Feature or Accessory to add to the Appeal score. A Trainer may not benefit from multiple pieces of Type Tailor that add to the same Contest Stat at the same time. In addition, Trainers wearing clothing made with Style Tailor get +1 when rolling a check that consults SDEF.

Combat Focus.effect: Add your ATK and DEF modifier to the damage you deal as a trainer.

Martial Endurance.effect: Add half your ATK and DEF modifiers and multiply the total by 5 HP. Add this to your Hit Point total.

Brick Break.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Double Kick.prerequisites: Martial Artist, a Pokémon who knows Double Kick
Double Kick.frequency: Daily – Every 4 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Façade.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Focus Punch.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Improved Combat Focus.effect: Add twice your ATK and DEF modifier to the damage you deal as a Trainer. This Feature replaces Combat Focus.

Improved Martial Endurance.effect: Add your ATK and DEF modifiers and multiply the total by 5 HP. Add this to your HP total. This Feature replaces Martial Endurance.

Jump Kick.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Mega Punch.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

C’mon and Slam.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
C’mon and Slam.effect: Use the move Slam. If you hit, the next ally to target that enemy may ignore any Evasion and other effects that modify AC checks on the target.

Run, Baby, Run!.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Run, Baby, Run!.effect: Add your ATK modifier to your Overland, Surface, or Underwater Skill until the end of your next turn. Run, Baby, Run! may be activated at the beginning of your turn, and only once per round. Athlete Features Comeback Kid Prerequisites: Athlete Daily Target: Pokémon or Trainers Effect: Use the move Retaliate.

Defensive Line.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Defensive Line.trigger: Your Pokémon is hit by an attack.
Defensive Line.effect: Once per round, if you have not already shifted, you may roll to intercept an attack for one of your Pokémon. Roll 1d20. If your Attack stat is 20 or below, you must roll 11 or better on the check you may shift into the way of the attack. If your Attack stat exceeds 20, you need only roll 6 or better on the check. If your Attack stat exceeds 30, you do not need to make a check. Dodge,Dip,Duck,Dive,andDodge Prerequisites: Athlete, 18 DEF Daily Effect: Use the move Detect.

Double Play.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Double Play.trigger: Your Pokémon hits with a Damage dealing Melee move.
Double Play.effect: If your Pokémon hits a foe that you dealt damage to this turn, it deals an additional X damage and pushes the target X meters, where X is its Power Skill. If the target is pushed into Blocking Terrain, the target takes an additional 2d12 damage. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, both take 1d12 damage.

Gotta Go Fast.effect: Use the move Extremespeed. You may not move more than 5 meters while using Extremespeed.

In the Zone.effect: Whenever you use Run, Baby, Run! You may use your Attack or Defense, instead of your Speed, to calculate your Initiative and Evasion until the end of your next turn.

Overtime.trigger: You are lowered to 0 HP or less, but not greater than -100% HP.
Overtime.effect: Before fainting, you may make one last shift, and perform a Trainer Action targeting an enemy.

Strength Training.target: A Pokémon with Power 5 or greater who is levelling up to a level evenly divisible by 5
Strength Training.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK modifier. If the total exceeds 15, the Pokémon learns Strength.

Turn It Up.effect: Any time you would use your SPD modifier you may use your ATK modifier instead.

Welcome to the Jam.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Welcome to the Jam.effect: Slam’s AC becomes AC4. If you successfully hit a foe that dealt damage to another of your allies this round or last round, the next time an allied Trainer of Pokémon would damage that foe they deal an additional Xd6 damage, where X is half of your ATK modifier.

Work that Body.effect: Choose Power or a Movement Skill that the target has at 4 or higher. Permanently increase the chosen Skill by 1. The Power Skill may not be raised to 7 or higher in this manner. A Movement Skill may not be raised more than three times this way with Work that Body.

Sensei.effect: Choose a specific Martial Art. You are a master of that form of Martial Arts and can perform stunts related without making checks. You may take Sensei as another form of Martial Art again as a secondary feature. Your Unarmed attacks have an AC of 2, and you may deal either Fighting or Normal Type damage with your Unarmed Attacks. Black Belt Features Circle Throw Prerequisites: Black Belt, Go Away! Daily Target: Pokémon or Trainers Effect: Use the move Circle Throw.

Double-Edge.frequency: Daily - Every 10 Levels gained, you may use this move another time per day

Go Away!.effect: Push the target X Meters where X is equal to your ATK modifier. If the target is pushed into Blocking Terrain, it takes 2d10 damage. If the target is pushed into another Legal Target, both take 1d10.

High Jump Kick.frequency: Daily - Every 5 Levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
High Jump Kick.effect: Use the Move High Jump Kick.

I Can Take It.effect: When taking damage from a move that you have suffered before, reduce the damage by your ATK modifier plus your DEF modifier. This may not reduce damage that was taken in order to use a Feature.

Low Sweep.frequency: Daily - Every 8 Levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day

Mach Punch.prerequisites: Black Belt, has suffered a Mach Punch attack
Mach Punch.frequency: Daily - Every 5 Levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Reckless.effect: When using a Move that can deal recoil damage to the user, you may choose to be treated as if you have the Ability Reckless. Declare this Feature before rolling Accuracy Check if you intend to use it.

Rolling Kick.frequency: Daily - Every 7 Levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day

Strong Guy.effect: Whenever you make an Attack check, or make a roll that requires you to add your ATK modifier to your roll for damage, add 3 to your roll.

Toughest Skin.effect: When you take damage from any source other than recoil, reduce the damage by your DEF modifier doubled unless the damage source specifically says it cannot be reduced. This Feature replaces Tough Skin.

Alacrity.effect: Add half of your ATK modifier to your Movement Speeds and your Speed Stat. You may treat Rough Terrain as Normal Terrain. You may run on vertical surfaces for up to your ATK modifier in meters before jumping off. You may also move silently without making checks.

Poison Powder.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Poison Powder.effect: Use the move Poison Powder. You may choose to use it as a Melee move without the Blast keyword.

Aerial Assault.frequency: Daily - Every 4 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Ally Switch.target: An Ally within 5 meters whom you have line of sight to.

Antidotes Master.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your DEF modifier. If the total is 13 or higher, the target is cured of their Poisoning. This effect succeeds automatically if you inflicted the poison.

Blur.effect: Attacks, Moves and Features targeting you that don’t require an Accuracy Check now require one, as though they had Accuracy Check of 2 + your DEF modifier until the end of the encounter.

Caltrops.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day Target: Any spot on the battlefield Effect: Use the Move Spikes or Poison Spikes
Caltrops.effect: Use the Move Spikes or Poison Spikes.

Deadly Poisons.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Deadly Poisons.effect: The target is Badly Poisoned instead. Double Team Prerequisites: Ninja, Blur, 15 SPD Daily Target: Self. Effect: Use the Move Double Team.

Poison Jab.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day

Sleep Powder.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Sleep Powder.effect: Use the Move Sleep Powder as a melee attack. Sleep Powder does not have the Blast keyword while used this way.

Smoke Bomb.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Smoke Bomb.effect: Use the move Smokescreen. Additionally, your Accuracy is never modified by a Smokescreen.

Vanish.effect: You may blend with your surrounding objects. You are invisible to others as long as you stay still, if you move, it takes 5 seconds to regain the camouflage with surroundings. Venoshock Prerequisites: Ninja, Vile Coating Daily Effect: Use the Move Venoshock.

Vile Coating.effect: You have the ability Poison Touch.

Master’s Expertise.effect: The base Accuracy Check for any Weapon of Choice is AC2. This does not apply to Weapon of Choice (Unarmed).

Power Attack.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Power Attack.trigger: You hit with an Arms Attack.
Power Attack.effect: Raise the Damage Base of your Arms Attack with your Weapon of Choice by +2. Weapon Masters have two basic triggers for the majority of their features; a roll of 16-20 or declaring an arms attack with a weapon (not an unarmed attack). You may only apply one feature per trigger. Note that Power Attack triggers upon a successful hit, and thus does not conflict with either of these triggers.

Barrage.prerequisites: Weapons Master, 17 ATK, 4 Weapons Master Features
Barrage.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Barrage.trigger: You declare an Arms Attack with a weapon.
Barrage.effect: You must be wielding a ranged weapon to use this Feature. Your next arms attack gains the Blast keyword. The blast radius in meters is equal to half of your ATK modifier.

Master’s Aegis.effect: While holding a weapon you gain 5 Defense for damage reduction.

Passing Blade.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels, you may perform this Feature another time per day Trigger: You declare an Arms attack with a weapon
Passing Blade.trigger: You declare an Arms attack with a weapon.
Passing Blade.effect: When using a melee weapon, you grant your attack the Pass keyword. If you are wielding a Ranged weapon, the attack is instead a column 1 meter wide, and 15 meters long.

Piercing Shot.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Piercing Shot.trigger: You roll 16-20 with an Arms Feature with a weapon Effect: Reduce the Arms Attack’s target’s Defense and Special Defense combat stages by 1.
Piercing Shot.effect: Reduce the Arms Attack’s target’s Defense and Special Defense combat stages by 1.

Sword of Sun and Moon.prerequisites: Weapons Master, 16 ATK, 5 Weapons Master Features
Sword of Sun and Moon.frequency: Daily - Every 20 levels, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Sword of Sun and Moon.trigger: You declare an Arms attack with a Weapon.
Sword of Sun and Moon.effect: Your next Arms Attack cannot miss, and deals Damage against the target’s Special Defense rather than Defense.

Razor Gash.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Razor Gash.trigger: You roll 16-20 with an Arms Feature with a Weapon.

Reaper’s Maneuver.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Reaper’s Maneuver.trigger: You roll 16-20 with an Arms Feature with a Weapon.

Weapons Dance.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Weapons Dance.trigger: You hit with an Unarmed arms attack Effect: You flinch the target.
Weapons Dance.effect: Use the Move Swords Dance. Your next Arms Attack has Scatter. YOGI Advanced Feature Prerequisites: Martial Artist, 14 DEF The Yogi has found inner peace and wishes to pass on their knowledge to others. The Yogi’s knowledge of pressure points helps them to relax allies and harm foes. A lesson with a Yogi can calm your mind and prepare you for anything. Pick 1 Skill: Healing, Sooth Base Features Yoga Break Yogi Feature At-Will Target: A Pokémon. Effect: After five minutes of work, the target is temporarily at full happiness and appears more appealing. If they level up without a negative incident within an hour of Yoga Break and have met all previous requirements for evolution, evolution is guaranteed. Pressure Points Yogi Feature Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Trigger: You hit with an Unarmed arms attack Effect: You flinch the target. If the target is successfully flinched, the target becomes immune to further uses of Pressure Points for the rest of the encounter. Yogi Features Boost Therapy Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi, Invigorating Routine Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Trigger: You use Yoga Break on a Trainer. Effect: Choose a stat. The target of Yoga Break gains +2 to the chosen stat’s modifier for 12 hours. A Trainer may only benefit from one instance of Boost Therapy at a time. Hand Burst Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Target: Pokémon or Trainers. Effect: Use the Move Arm Thrust. If the same target is hit 4 times in the same round by Arm Thrust, used with Hand Burst, the target is Paralyzed. Healing Touch Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi, 16 DEF Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Target: Pokémon or Trainers that are not in an encounter. Effect: After ten minutes of work, the target regains HP equal to 2d12+10 plus your DEF modifier. Impose Limberness Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi, Limber Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Target: Any adjacent Paralyzed or Flinched Target. Effect: The target is cured of their Paralysis and Flinched status. Invigorating Routine Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Trigger: You use Yoga Break Effect: The target of Yoga Break gains temporary HP equal to your Defense Stat. In addition, you may now target other Trainers with Massage Therapy. Limber Prerequisites: Yogi Static Effect: You are immune to Paralysis. Yogi Feature Nerve Strike Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi, Hand Burst Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Effect: Use an Unarmed Arms Attack. On hit, you paralyze the target. Precisionist Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi Static Effect: Whenever you make a Speed check, or make a roll that requires you to add your SPD modifier to your roll for damage, add 5 to your roll. Relaxation Therapy Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi, Invigorating Routine Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Trigger: You use Yoga Break Effect: The target of Yoga Break gains the ability ‘Inner Focus’ for their next encounter that day. Smelling Salts Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi, Hand Burst Daily – Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Target: Pokémon or Trainers. Effect: Use the Move Smelling Salts. Stretch Therapy Yogi Feature Prerequisites: Yogi, Invigorating Routine Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day. Trigger: You use Yoga Break Effect: The target of Massage Therapy gains the ability ‘Limber’ for the next encounter that day.

Calming Vibe.effect: You calm the target, curing it of Confusion. Calming Vibe can calm rages, focus a distorted mind, or neutralize a paranoid panic. In order to use Calming Vibe, the Trainer must be in a calm state of mind. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Light Healing Power.frequency: Daily – Every 4 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Light Healing Power.effect: Roll 1d12+6 and add twice your SATK modifier. The target heals that many Hit Points. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Bonds.effect: While unconscious, your Pokémon can act on their accord as if you are not unconscious. You may only have as many Pokémon acting per round of an encounter as if you were conscious.

Connection.prerequisites: Empath, 4 Pokémon you’ve levelled up through training 15 levels
Connection.effect: You understand any owned Pokémon’s thoughts.

Digging.effect: You may read thoughts from the past hour in a mind with Telepathy. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Healing Power.frequency: Daily – Every 6 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Healing Power.effect: Roll 2d12+12 and add twice your SATK modifier. The target heals that many Hit Points. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Heal the Body.prerequisites: Empath, 16 SATK, Cured Burn and/or Poison three times with items or features
Heal the Body.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Heal the Body.effect: Cures the target of Burn and Poison. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Mend the Mind.prerequisites: Empath, 16 SATK, Cured Paralysis and/or Sleep three times with items or features
Mend the Mind.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Mend the Mind.effect: Cures the target of Paralysis and Sleep. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Major Healing Power.frequency: Daily – Every 12 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Major Healing Power.effect: Roll 3d12+18 and add your SATK stat. The target heals that many Hit Points. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Natural Connection.effect: You understand Pokémon’s thoughts. This Feature replaces Connection.

Serious Healing Power.frequency: Daily – Every 12 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Serious Healing Power.effect: Roll 4d12+24 and add your SATK stat. The target heals that many Hit Points. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Pain Share.target: A Pokémon or Trainer taking damage.
Pain Share.effect: You may half the damage the target takes. If you do, subtract the amount of damage you prevent from your HP, then subtract another 5 HP from your HP for each time you’ve used Pain Share during that day before this instance.

Pain Split.effect: Use the Move Pain Split. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Rest.effect: After ten minutes of focus, you recover your full HP.

Trying On Shoes.effect: You may read thoughts from the past day in a mind with Telepathy. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Wish.effect: Use the Move Wish. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Glare.effect: Use the Move Glare. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Mental Suggestion.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Mental Suggestion.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 20, you may place a suggestion into the target’s mind. The target doesn’t need to act on it, nor do they immediately know why they thought of it. Hex

Confuse Ray.prerequisites: Hex Maniac, a Pokémon who knows the Move Confuse Ray
Confuse Ray.effect: Use the Move Confuse Ray. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Curse.prerequisites: Hex Maniac, a Pokémon who knows the Move Curse
Curse.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Curse.effect: Use the Move Curse as if you are Ghost Type. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Dream Eater.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Dream Eater.effect: Use the Move Dream Eater. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue. Fake Tears Prerequisites: Hex Maniac At-Will - Drains 8 of user’s HP per use. Target: Pokémon or Trainers. Effect: Use the Move Fake Tears.

Fumble.effect: Force the target to reroll any successful check. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue. For every time you use this ability per day, past the first use on any day, your GM gets one Murphy’s Law point. They may use this point to have you reroll any die you did not target with Fumble, the GM may only target your dice rolls with Murphy’s Law Points. Hex Prerequisites: Hex Maniac At-Will - Drains 15 of user’s HP per use. Target: Pokémon or Trainers. Effect: Use the Move Hex.

Mind Control.prerequisites: Hex Maniac, 26 SATK, Level 18, Now to Act
Mind Control.effect: You have control of the target entirely. You know what they know and can have them do anything they can do. If you use mind control for more than 30 seconds at a time, you drain 40 HP every 6 seconds. Anyone may try to break out from Mind Control by making a Will check as a Free Action. The Check is to roll equal to or greater than the difference between the Mind Controller’s Special Attack and the target’s Special Attack on 1d20, adding the target’s SATK modifier. If the check is successful, the Mind Controller does not lose the 25, or 40 HP they would have if they were successful in Mind Controlling the target. If the target breaks free of Mind Control, they may not be targeted by Mind Control again for at least 48 hours. The target may attempt the Will check once per round. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Now to Act.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If the total exceeds 15, the target will act on a suggestion on their mind. If the suggestion is entirely contradictory to their regular behavior, they will be hesitant throughout the execution of the suggestion. If it isn’t possible to execute the suggestion, they will fail trying. They may make one Will check to avoid executing the suggestion. They must roll above 16 on 1d20 adding the target’s SATK modifier. Using this on a target leaves discoverable psychic residue.

Commander’s Demeanor.effect: Your experiences under heavy stress and crises situations has made you a natural leader. Whenever you make a check related to communication with others, add +5 to your roll. You must still use your SDEF stat as usual.

Encourage.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Encourage.effect: If the target successfully deals damage to an enemy on their next turn and you dealt damage to that same enemy on your last turn, they deal additional damage equal to your ATK or SPD modifier.

Commander’s Rally.prerequisites: Commander, a Ranger in your party
Commander’s Rally.effect: All allied trainers within sight or hearing range of you gain +3 to all modifiers for X rounds, where X is equal to your ATK modifier. This Feature may only be in effect once at any given time.

Herder.effect: You may make two Restraint attempts as one Trainer Action. You must target two, adjacent Pokémon when using Herder.

Inspiring Presence.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Inspiring Presence.effect: Use immediately after dealing damage to an enemy. The target gains Temporary HP equal to twice your SPD modifier until the end of the encounter.

Leader’s Guidance.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Leader’s Guidance.effect: Target allied Trainer may immediately perform any trainer action or feature that has a frequency of At-Will.

Master Befriender.effect: You may give the Helpful title to two additional Pokémon. Upon taking this Feature you now can control up to 7 Helpful Pokémon. You may still have your Companion in addition to the 7 Helpful Pokémon you may Restrain.

On My Signal.frequency: Daily - every 8 levels you may use this Feature another time per day
On My Signal.effect: Use immediately after dealing damage to an enemy. If the target makes an accuracy roll on their next turn, they add half of your ATK modifier to their Accuracy Roll.

Pull Through!.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Pull Through!.trigger: An Ally makes a roll due to a status condition.
Pull Through!.effect: The Triggering Ally gets a +X Bonus on any rolls made due to Paralysis, Sleep, Frozen, Confusion, or Infatuation that turn, where X is your ATK modifier.

Requested Assistance.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels you may use this Feature another time per day
Requested Assistance.target: Any Route, Town, City, Mountain, etc.
Requested Assistance.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK modifier, if the total is higher than 13 you locate a Pokémon who has a Skill of your choice, if choosing a Skill with a value, the value may not be higher than 6. If choosing a Speed Skill, it may not be any higher than 12. If the total is higher than 18, you may pick another Skill that the Pokémon will have. Any attempt at attacking or capturing the Pokémon results in the Pokémon fleeing without fail. The Pokémon must still be made Helpful or Companion to use.

Stand Up!.trigger: An Ally Trainer with over 1HP is reduced to 0HP or lower Effect: That Ally’s HP is reduced to 1 instead.
Stand Up!.effect: That Ally’s HP is reduced to 1 instead. This ability may be used as intercept, and only once per

Tactician.prerequisites: Commander, Inspiring Presence, Leader’s Guidance, On My Signal!
Tactician.frequency: Daily - Every 25 levels you may use this Feature another time per day
Tactician.effect: Choose two of Encourage, At My Signal!, Inspiring Presence, or Leader’s Guidance. The target gains the effects of both chosen features. Use of Tactician depletes a daily use of Tactician as well as each of the chosen features.

Conspiracy Beater.effect: When in an encounter or facing any criminal or criminal suspect, your Stat modifiers are raised by 1.

Intuition’s Arsenal.effect: When you activate this Feature, choose and gain one of the following Abilities permanently: Forewarn, Frisk, Infiltrator, Insomnia, Inner Focus, Keen Eye, Pickpocket, Unaware, or Unnerve.

Disguise.effect: After ten minutes of work, you are unrecognizable. You may not impersonate anyone with Disguise.

Detective Training.target: Your Pokémon or Companion with Loyalty 3 or higher.
Detective Training.effect: Mark your Pokémon as an “Investigator”. Your Pokémon with Investigator gain +1 Intelligence or have their Intelligence increased to 4, whichever is higher. Additionally, the target gains one of the following Skills: Stealth, Tracker, or Mind Lock.

Intuition Training.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Intuition Training.effect: The target gains a Pokémon ability that you have gained through Intuition’s Arsenal, in addition to the abilities they already have. If the Pokémon already has the chosen ability, this feature has no effect. This effect ends when the Pokémon is recalled or after they rest for the day.

Evidence Search.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Evidence Search.target: A crime scene, suspect’s home or office, or any area that may contain material relevant to your current situation.
Evidence Search.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK or SPD modifier to the roll. If you roll 15 or higher, you find a piece of evidence, if available, related to what you are looking for, even if its relevance is not immediately obvious. In addition, you get a +5 bonus to any Investigate Skill Checks.

Lie Detector.target: A person that you suspect may be willing to lie.
Lie Detector.effect: When you declare the use of this feature, specify a statement made by the target and you will immediately know if it is completely truthful. This feature may be used on recorded information. If the target is saying something that they believe is true, then they do not appear to be lying.

Master of Disguise.effect: After one hour of work, you may make a disguise that looks like a specific person. You can disguise yourself as other people, Pokémon or even inanimate objects of your size and be unrecognizable as a fake to the eyes of everyone around you. It takes 10 minutes to put on a disguise. You may pick two disguises to have on hand, and may leave additional disguises you make at Ranger Stations. This Feature replaces Disguise.

Partner’s Aid.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Partner’s Aid.effect: Using this feature gives up your Command Action for the round. You command your Pokémon to aid you in your current actions, giving you a +3 Bonus to any rolls made when using a Detective Feature. If Conspiracy Beater is currently applicable, it may target any roll. Your Pokémon marked as “Investigator” must be able to act and be nearby.

Smooth Talker.effect: Whenever you make a Special Attack or Special Defense check, or make a roll that requires you to add your SATK or SDEF modifier, add 5 to your roll.

Stop Right There!.trigger: You are pursuing a fleeing target or in an encounter with an enemy you have been searching for.
Stop Right There!.effect: Add half your ATK modifier to your Speed and your SPD modifier to your Attack for 5 minutes. This does not affect modifiers further.

What’s That Over There.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
What’s That Over There.target: A person not in combat and not hostile to you, or unaware of your presence.
What’s That Over There.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK or SPD modifier. If you roll above the target’s SATK or SDEF stat, you are successfully able to distract them with a trick or a menial chore for X minutes, where X is equal to the difference between their chosen stat and your roll’s result. If they have more pressing business or are otherwise alerted, they will stop whatever it is they were doing earlier than normal, if not immediately.

Stick to It.effect: You automatically succeed on all checks to remain on your mount caused by attacks and status conditions. You can ride your mounts on their side, while hanging upside down from them, while standing on them or any other odd way of staying on your mount as if you are riding the mount as normal.

Battle Capable Riding.frequency: Daily - Every 5 Levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Battle Capable Riding.trigger: You are hit by an attack while riding a Pokémon.
Battle Capable Riding.effect: Your mount is hit by the attack instead of yourself. If you would have both been hit, your mount may take both sets of damage.

All-Terrain Mount.prerequisites: Rider, a Pokémon with Stealth, Phasing, Groundshaper or Icestep
All-Terrain Mount.effect: While mounted, your mount may ignore the effects of Rough Terrain and is treated as if it has the Icestep Skill.

Charge!.frequency: Daily - Every 25 Levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Charge!.effect: If you are mounted, for the rest of the round, all attacks you make that deal Physical Damage have the Dash keyword, and do additional damage equal to your Mount’s relevant movement Skill.

Lightriding.effect: While you are mounted, if you are the only person on your mount, your mount does not suffer the usual penalties to Movement Skills and Speed Combat Stages. Additionally, while you are mounted, your mount may carry one more person than it usually could regardless of its Power score or body shape.

Mental Crop.prerequisites: Rider, a Pokémon with Overland Speed 8 or higher Skills
Mental Crop.effect: While you are mounted, your mount gains a +X Bonus to their Movement Skills, and a +1 Bonus to its Jump Skill X is equal to your SPD modifier.

Mobility.effect: Instead of using your SPD Stat to determine your Initiative during trainer turns, you may choose to use your mount’s Speed Stat. You still act during trainer turns as normal.

Mounted Quick Switch.effect: You can switch out Pokémon you are mounted on to another Pokémon from your Poke Balls without dismounting, even if your mount is in motion. You may even switch the type of mount while it is moving.

Pure Speed.prerequisites: Rider, a Pokémon with 25 Speed or higher
Pure Speed.effect: Your mount, that you are currently riding, has its Speed stat raised by two Combat Stages.

Qualified Ride.frequency: Daily - Every 5 Levels gained, you may use this Feature another time per day
Qualified Ride.effect: You may mount a willing Pokémon as a Free Action during your turn, and doing so does not forfeit the Pokémon’s Shift Action or Move Action that round.

Ride Pager.effect: When making a Wild appear through use of a Ranger Feature or any Advanced Feature, you may choose to specify that the Wild is capable of being ridden.

Watch and Learn.effect: The target may benefit from up to two Rider Features you have with Static Frequencies while within 3 meters of you. Only one ally may benefit from Watch and Learn at a time. These benefits last until the ally is no longer in range, is no longer mounted, or receives different benefits.

Food Scavenger.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Food Scavenger.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier. If the total exceeds 12, you find enough food and water for a whole day for up to seven humans and/or Pokémon.

Internalized Senses.effect: At any time, you can discern the direction you are facing. You know a complete forecast of the day’s natural weather. You know a rough estimate of the time of day, to the nearest quarter of an hour.

Acid Rain.target: A Pokémon using a Move with the Weather keyword.
Acid Rain.effect: When the Pokémon uses a Move with the Weather keyword, instead of replacing a Weather effect already on the field, both Weather effects simultaneously exist on the field. If a new Weather effect is placed on the field after the two that are out, both are replaced by the third.

Bring Him Down!.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Bring Him Down!.trigger: You declare an Arms attack with a ranged weapon.
Bring Him Down!.effect: On a successful hit, you paralyze the target. If you roll higher than 15 on the AC, this attack Flinches the target as well.

Forecast.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Forecast.target: Any Route, Town, City, Mountain, etc.
Forecast.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier, if the total is higher than 18 you locate a Pokémon who can perform one of the following moves of your choice: Sunny Day, Rain Dance, Hail, Sandstorm or Defog. Any attempt at attacking or capturing the Pokémon results in the Pokémon fleeing without fail. The Pokémon must still be made Helpful or Companion to use.

Freeclimber.effect: You do not need to make any checks while moving through rough terrain and move through rough terrain at your normal speed. You do not need to make any checks while climbing anything. You may climb at your Overland Speed -3.

Huntsman.effect: Targets of your ranged arms attacks gain no speed evasion bonus, gain no bonuses from Moves or effects that say, ‘you must roll +X during Accuracy Checks,’ and no evasion bonuses from terrain or weather. In addition, if you are not a Martial Artist, or an Advanced Class with a Martial Artist prerequisite, your ranged arms attacks deal additional damage and range, equal to half your Speed.

Master Gyver.target: Wood, Bone, Plastic, and any other workable material with a knife
Master Gyver.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your ATK and SPD modifiers, if the total is higher than 18, you can create any non-electronic tool/gadget/etc. within reason. You are a master of ingenuity and can create onetime use contraptions to solve your various problems. You can also create partially hidden shelter from the surrounding area large enough for 5 people and/or Pokémon, which will last X days, X being your SPD modifier. More than 2 shelters close together become easy to see by any passing Pokémon or human.

Pacifism.frequency: Daily - Every 6 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Pacifism.effect: If you successfully restrain the Pokémon, you may not use the Helpful Pokémon to attack anything, but you have -15 added to your restrain roll.

Watch the Sky.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Watch the Sky.trigger: There is a weather in effect.
Watch the Sky.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SPD modifier, if the total is higher than 10 you temporarily gain the Overcoat Pokémon Ability. If the result is higher than 15, you and all your Pokémon, may temporarily gain the Overcoat Pokémon Ability. If the result is higher than 25, you, all your Pokémon, your allies and their Pokémon, may temporarily gain the Overcoat Pokémon Ability. If the weather changes, or Acid Rain is used, the protection fails and must be reapplied as a trainer action, with no additional roll needed during that encounter.

Wilderness Rester.effect: Whenever you rest in the wild for at least 8 hours, you wake up at full HP, cured of any afflictions. If you are suffering from diseases, have any broken bones or open wounds, these are not healed.

Frantic Research.frequency: Daily – Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Frantic Research.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 15, you learn what level the Pokémon is. If you roll higher then 20, you identify how much HP the target currently has.

Walking Encyclopedia.target: Anything related to knowledge you can look up.
Walking Encyclopedia.effect: You know Pokémon data, relating to Pokémon in your Pokédex, about move sets, type, type advantage, evolution methods, abilities, height, weight, and location. You have access to all information a Pokédex would grant, immediately.

Did the Homework.effect: Spend an hour doing research about the target. Every 12 levels gained, you may research an additional target during this hour. The GM gives you X Insight Points for the target that last until the end of the day, where X is your SATK or SDEF modifier. While near the target and taking actions related to that target for the rest of the day, you may spend an Insight Point once per round as a free action to treat either your SATK or SDEF mod as 3 higher for the duration of that round. When you spend an Insight Point, your GM gives you a fact about the target.

Field Commander.effect: You may issue commands to Pokémon who has not already acted on a round of an encounter. There must be understood consent from the Pokémon’s trainer unless they are unconscious. Pokémon may only act once per round of an encounter. You still may only issue one command per round unless you have Dual Wielding.

Frantic Research +.frequency: Daily – Every 3 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Frantic Research +.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 15, you learn what level the Pokémon is and at least 6 of its attacks that a standard one of its species would not naturally have at its level. If it has fewer than 6 attacks meeting this criterion, you learn all of them. If you roll higher then 20, you identify how much HP the target currently has. This Feature replaces Frantic Research.

Game Plan.frequency: Daily – Every 6 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Game Plan.effect: Shift the target up to X meters where X is your SATK modifier or your SDEF modifier.

Game Plan +.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Game Plan +.effect: Shift all allies and their Pokémon up to X meters where X is your SATK modifier or your SDEF modifier. Game Plan + does not replace Game Plan.

Read the Manual.effect: You know the function of any machine. Additionally, you know the function of the item if it is a standard item with an effect given in the Player Handbook. You know of any defects that might change the way the item normally functions.

Repel Crafting.effect: After one hour of work, create a Repel. Name the concoction whatever you’d like out of whatever ingredients you’d like.

Strategic Targeting.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Strategic Targeting.effect: Add half your SDEF modifier to the target’s accuracy roll when they make an attack this turn.

Tactical Response.effect: Choose any Move and then pick one No Damage - Self Move known by the target. For the rest of the day when your targeted Pokémon is active and a foe uses the first Move chosen, it may immediately use the chosen No Damage - Self Move as an interrupt one time. This does not consume your command for the turn.

Trait Theory.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 10, you identify what Nature the Pokémon has. If you roll higher than 15, you also identify what Abilities the Pokémon has.

Trait Theory +.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 10, you identify what Nature the Pokémon has. If you roll higher than 15, you also identify what Abilities the Pokémon has. If the target is Wild and you rolled higher then 20, you may pick the target’s Nature. This Feature Replaces Trait Theory.

Praise.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Praise.effect: Roll d20 and add your SATK and SDEF modifiers to the roll. If you roll higher then 15, the target favors you and will hesitate to attack you and your Pokémon. The Legendary will also like you more.

Walking Mythos.target: Anything related to knowledge you can look up.
Walking Mythos.effect: You know Pokémon data, relating to Pokémon in your Pokédex, about move sets, type, type advantage, evolution methods, abilities, height, weight, and location. Restricted to Legendary Pokémon you have encountered.

Beckon.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, used Praise on at least one Legendary
Beckon.target: A Legendary Pokémon’s habitat of a Legendary you’ve met.
Beckon.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 20, a Legendary Pokémon will appear. It will not necessarily appear near you, but you will be able to see it, or evidence of its presence.

Blessing.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, allied with a Legendary Pokémon
Blessing.frequency: Daily – Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Blessing.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier doubled. The target recovers HP equal to the total rolled. You may not use Blessing if you are no longer allied with any Legendary Pokémon.

Channel Divinity.effect: The target may perform a non-Signature Move that is known by a Legendary Pokémon you are Linked with. This counts as Issuing a Command. This may only be used once per turn.

God Speak.prerequisites: Professor, Linguist, allied with a Legendary Pokémon
God Speak.effect: You can speak to and understand all Legendary Pokémon.

Guardian Link.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, used Praise on at least one Legendary
Guardian Link.effect: You may only target Legendary Pokémon with Guardian Link if you are allied with them. The Legendary becomes Linked. While a Linked Legendary is in danger, regardless of where you are in the world, you will know.

Legendary Blessing.effect: Roll 2d20 and add your SDEF modifier tripled. The target recovers HP equal to the total rolled. You may not use Legendary Blessing if you are no longer allied with any Legendary Pokémon.

Link Summon.target: A Linked Legendary, disregarding any line of sight.
Link Summon.effect: You may summon the allied, Linked Legendary Pokémon. It will behave as it sees fit.

Miracle.trigger: Allied Trainer or Allied Pokémon fails a Death Savings Throw.
Miracle.effect: The target may re-roll that Death Savings throw and subtract 5 from their roll if you target a trainer or subtract 25 if you target a Pokémon.

Morale Blessing.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, Field Commander, allied with a Legendary Pokémon
Morale Blessing.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Morale Blessing.effect: Pick either Attack, Defense, Special Attack, Special Defense or Speed and raise the chosen stat of each of your ally’s Pokémon’s within X meters of you by 1 Combat Stage until the end of the encounter. X is your SDEF modifier.

Omen.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Class Feature another time per day
Omen.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 10, you learn of the last time a Legendary Pokémon has been around the area.

Soul Searching.prerequisites: Cryptozoologist, Linked to a Legendary Pokémon
Soul Searching.target: A Linked Legendary, disregarding any line of sight.
Soul Searching.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 13, you are pointed in the direction of the target. If the total exceeds 17, you learn of the distance between the Linked Legendary and yourself.

Digging for the Past.effect: After thirty minutes of work, roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you roll higher than 15, you either find clues to fossils that are nearby or evidence of the area’s history. If you roll higher than 20 you choose to find either a piece of the area’s history or a fossil, both are chosen by your GM after you specify.

Stone Search.frequency: Daily – Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Stone Search.effect: After thirty minutes of work, Roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you roll higher than 20, you find an Evolutionary stone chosen by your GM. Mechanics: See the Fossil Reanimation Rules determining how Fossils are reanimated.

Adventuring Archeologist.effect: You have begun to dabble in exploring ruins as well as caves. You know your regions history at a college level. You can sell or donate any pieces of history you find to museums while Digging for the Past for X, where X is 1d20+SATK mod times 30.

Dig, Dig, Dig, Dug.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Dig, Dig, Dig, Dug.effect: Over the course of five minutes you dig a 5-meter-wide and 2-meter-deep pit. The Pit Trap may be covered by camouflage that Pokémon or Trainers can’t distinguish from the surroundings. You can either choose to activate the trap from nearby or to have it activate when 100 pounds of pressure is put onto the camouflage.

Dust Settling.effect: After two minutes of work, modify your Pit Trap so that when something falls into your Pit Trap, use the Move SandAttack on the victim.

How to Ruin a Gneiss Day.frequency: Daily - Every 15 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
How to Ruin a Gneiss Day.effect: After ten minutes of work, modify your Pit Trap so that when something falls into your Pit Trap, use the Move Rock Slide on the victim.

Elemental Trapping.effect: After two minutes of work, graft an Evolution Stone into the Pit Trap. When the trap is activated use the Move Rock Slide, but instead of treating it as a Rock-Type Move, treat it like a move according to the type of Elemental Stone grafted onto the Pit Trap. Fire Stones deal Fire Type Damage; Leaf Stones, Grass; Thunder Stone, Electric; Water Stones, Water; Ice Stone, Ice; and Stones created through Stone Polishing do damage of their assigned type. The Stone is destroyed after use.

Movie Magic Quicksand.frequency: Daily - Every 10 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Movie Magic Quicksand.effect: After ten minutes of work, modify your Pit Trap so that when something falls into your Pit Trap, use the Move Sand Tomb on the victim. The victims are stuck for 1d4 turns unless they have an Underground Speed Skill.

Reanimator.effect: After five hours of work, build a one-time use Reanimator Kit. You may use it immediately to make a Reanimation check. When you make a check to Reanimate a fossilized Pokémon add 3 to the roll. Reanimators can reanimate fossils anywhere with a steady power supply with their Reanimation Kit and do not need to visit a specialized lab. If the Kit is not used within 24 hours, it falls apart.

Slated for Renovation.effect: After three hours of work, while underground, you set off a controlled explosion. You may set off the explosion from up to 30 meters away. If anyone is within 5 meters of the charge, you immediately use the Move Fire Blast on the victim. Roll 1d10 to determine additional effects from the detonation. 1: Cave in – Everyone within 30 meters of the charge in the cavern is automatically hit with the move Rock Tomb. 2: Natural Deterrent – If breaking into a new underground tunnel, it is partially blocked off by the appropriate natural event: underground spring, sand cave in, lava, or Pokémon interaction. 3: Wanton Destruction - Any items, fossils, or Pokémon are destroyed or scared off from the entire cave network. 4-9: Successful demolition 10: Discovery! - You find a buried site, details are left up to your GM.

Spelunking is the Schist.effect: While underground you know if there are other viable exits to the surface or geologically unstable areas. You may also find areas that grant a +3 bonus to your Digging for the Past and Stone Search Feature rolls.

Stone Energizer.effect: The stone gains a second use before being destroyed. You may not use Stone Energizer on the same stone twice.

Stone Polish.effect: After ten hours of work, you may turn that stone into an Evolution Stone. Choose one of the eighteen elemental types. When you touch a Pokémon with an Evolution Stone that matches one of their Elemental Types will forcefully evolve them if they meet the evolution level requirement but has not yet evolved.

The Good Doctor.target: A Pokémon deceased within the last 24 hours.
The Good Doctor.effect: After five hours of work, roll 1d20 and add your SATK modifier. If you roll higher than 20 you create an artificial fossil from the corpse of the Pokémon. This fossil acts as a normal fossil. You may not target Legendary Pokémon with this feature.

Snapshot.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Snapshot.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. Multiply the result by 20. This is the approximate value of the photo. You may try to sell this photograph anywhere.

Action Photo.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Action Photo.trigger: You or an ally are attacked by a Pokémon using a Move Effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier.
Action Photo.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, you successfully obtain a photo of the Move. Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. Multiply the total by 30. This is the approximate value of the photo. You may try to sell this photograph anywhere.

Action Photo +.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Action Photo +.effect: Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15, you successfully obtain a photo of the Move. Roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. Multiply the total by 45. This is the approximate value of the photo. You may try to sell this photograph anywhere.

Battle Study.effect: While you possess a photograph taken with Action Photo of a specific Move, any use of that Move will require +2 on an Accuracy Check to hit you or your Pokémon.

Camera Flash.effect: Use the Move Flash. You may use this feature as a Free Action when you use Snapshot, Action Photo, or Action Photo+, but must share the same target as that Feature.

Examine.frequency: Daily - Every 5 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Examine.trigger: You use Snapshot on a Pokémon or Trainer Effect: You may use the Move Foresight on Snapshot’s Target as a free action.
Examine.effect: You may use the Move Foresight on Snapshot’s Target as a free action. Alternatively, you may, as a Trainer Action, study a Photo you have already taken to use Foresight on the photo’s target.

Natural Shot.effect: When using snapshot on a wild Pokémon, instead of multiplying your Snapshot roll by 20, multiply the result by 45.

Pester Ball A.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
Pester Ball A.effect: After 6 seconds of work on your last turn, roll 1d20 and add your SDEF modifier. If the total exceeds 15 use the move Taunt on the target. Pester Ball A cannot target a Pokémon who has already been successfully hit with any Pester Ball during this encounter.

Pester Ball B.frequency: Daily - Every 8 levels gained, you may perform this Feature another time per day
